page: Hetzner Cloud cax41 is an arm64 virtual server powered by the Ampere Altra processor family, specifically utilizing the Neoverse-N1 model. It features 16 shared vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz, with 16 physical cores and a 1.0 thread-per-core topology. The instance is equipped with 31.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core, and includes 320 GB of local SSD storage. Virtualization is managed via the QEMU hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 29117.84 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8498.0. Single-core performance is weaker, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1034.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1821.99. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 1126.26 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 102.2 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, cryptographic and decompression tasks show some limitations, with a weak PassMark CPU encryption score of 2590.29 and a poor single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 7.08 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the cax41 offers a high density of physical cores and memory on an energy-efficient Arm architecture, making it a viable option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require dedicated CPU resources. It is well-suited for applications such as static web serving, which achieved a throughput of 16.72 GB/sec, Redis database operations, and software compilation. It is less suitable for single-threaded processing, heavy cryptographic tasks, or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Hetzner Cloud cax41 is an arm64 virtual server powered by a 16-core Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 31.0 GB of RAM, 320 GB of local SSD storage, and operates under the QEMU hypervisor with shared CPU allocation. Benchmarks show average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 29117.84 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8498.0, though single-core performance and cryptographic speeds are relatively weak. The server excels in memory bandwidth, delivering 1126.26 GB/sec for cached small blocks, and demonstrates solid throughput for static web serving at 16.72 GB/sec and Redis operations. This hardware profile provides a cost-efficient option for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and web serving, while being less optimal for single-threaded or heavy cryptographic tasks.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cax41 is an arm64 virtual server featuring 16 shared Ampere Altra cores, 31.0 GB of RAM, and 320 GB of SSD storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cax41 is an arm64 virtual server with 16 Ampere Altra cores, 31.0 GB RAM, and 320 GB SSD storage, managed via QEMU hypervisor.
tagline: An arm64 virtual server with sixteen shared cores and local SSD storage, optimized for multi-threaded applications and web serving.
bullet_points: 16 shared Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 arm64 cores running at 2.0 GHz
31.0 GB of system memory and 320 GB of local SSD storage
Average multi-core performance suitable for software compilation and Redis operations
High memory bandwidth of 1126.26 GB/sec for cached small blocks
Best suited for static web serving, multi-threaded workloads, and general-purpose Arm development
